target.name: Aurora Innovation
target.founder: Chris Urmson, Sterling Anderson, J. Andrew (Drew) Bagnell

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 2 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Joby Aviation
private.rationale: Same sector (Drones & Autonomous), same stage (Public), post-money $6.86B

private.name: Archer Aviation
private.rationale: Same sector (Drones & Autonomous), same stage (Public), post-money $3.33B

data.narrative:
Aurora Innovation operates in the Drones & Autonomous sector at the public stage, having raised approximately $2.5B to date. The comparable universe for this company is constrained: while five private peers exist within the sector—including Joby Aviation, Archer Aviation, Zipline, Nuro, and Elroy Air—no public comparables trade in this space. The peer set lacks sufficient depth for reliable multiples-based valuation.

Peer-median analysis is inapplicable here. The methodology requires a minimum of three same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables within a similar valuation tier; the Drones & Autonomous universe yielded only two same-stage peers and zero adjacent-stage or tier-similar peers meeting the filter. Relying on an all-sector median would introduce unacceptable noise given the specialized nature of autonomous vehicle development, capital intensity, and commercialization timelines.

Without applicable triangulation methodologies, a quantitative valuation range cannot be responsibly derived from the data provided. The analysis highlights a fundamental constraint: Aurora Innovation's position as a public-stage company in an emerging, sparsely-populated sector limits comparability-based approaches. Any valuation framework would require either (1) expanding to broader mobility or hardware sectors at the cost of relevance, or (2) deploying alternative methods such as discounted cash flow or precedent transaction analysis, neither of which are supported by the current dataset. Key uncertainties center on comparable company selection and the applicability of traditional public-market multiples to pre-revenue or early-revenue autonomous platforms.
